intro: Hygrophila parishii, also known as Hygrophila, is a tropical aquatic plant native to the Western Ghats of India, used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Hygrophila include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, treat infections, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hygrophila include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Hygrophila include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
